frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
generated_comment:
Rahul: Bas maa, poore week Swiggy aur mess ka khana khaya, pet bhar gaya but mann bilkul nahi bhara.
Mother: Arre, toh kuch achha bana liya karo na Sunday ko.
Rahul: Achha matlab tumhare haath jaisa kaun bana sakta hai, maa? Sach bolun, tumne itna standard high kar diya hai ki life mein koi aur waise khana bana hi nahi payegi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This sounds affectionate but is misogynistic because it idealizes the mother's cooking as the benchmark all other women are implicitly expected to meet. It encodes a covert gendered expectation that future female partners will be judged through domestic labor and comparison to maternal sacrifice.
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Bengaluru who moved from Patna three years ago. You are on a phone call with your mother on a Sunday evening. She has been alone at home all week. Your father is away on work. You are telling her about the food you ate this week Ñ mostly Swiggy orders and mess food. At some point in the conversation you say something about her cooking that sounds like love and gratitude but is actually a comparison that diminishes every other woman who will ever cook for you and sets a standard no one asked your mother to set and no one can meet.
